evidence_atoms.measurement_or_claim: Loss of PICALM reduces amyloid-beta clearance across blood brain barrier models and may increase vascular amyloid burden.

Safety, reversibility, and dosing schedules remain important review questions for clinical
translation.
Focused ultrasound BBB opening needs standardized safety and dosing evidence before broad Alzheimer use.

# PICALM and LRP1 at the blood brain barrier

PICALM regulates clathrin-dependent internalization of amyloid-beta bound to LRP1 in brain endothelial cells.

This fixture is a valid small PDF so Vela can exercise the PDF ingestion path without
storing a large binary.
Model studies suggest that transporter balance, endothelial injury, and inflammatory signaling can change amyloid-beta exposure in Alzheimer disease contexts.
LRP1 supports amyloid-beta clearance across the blood-brain barrier.
Blood-brain barrier transport mechanisms influence amyloid-beta movement between blood and brain compartments.
RAGE is associated with amyloid-beta influx into the brain across BBB endothelium.
These claims are source notes for a local fixture and require review against the cited paper before scientific use.

Focused ultrasound blood brain barrier opening in Alzheimer disease
Focused ultrasound can transiently open the blood brain barrier and may improve delivery of
therapeutic agents in Alzheimer disease models.
